LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY PROPER BUT IT IS DENIED BECAUSE IT REQUIRES THE COURT TO DETERMINE THE NATURE OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES AND THE APPLICABLE INSURANCE POLICY/POLICIES AND THESE ARE ISSUES BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE PLEADINGS. COURT'S RULINGS ON DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE INSURANCE POLICIES. THE ISSUE DEFENDANT SEEKS TO RAISE IS BEST ADDRESSED BY A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED IN THE TULARE COUNTY ACTION. COMBINED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR 12365

RULING: DISCUSSED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND GRANTS THE PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICAL NOTICE. COMBINED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR 12365

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY, AND, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, AND, OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE 2002-2003 POLICIES ARE THE ONLY POLICIES AT ISSUE. THE TRIGGER OF COVERAGE WAS THE SINGLE EVENT OF THE ACCIDENT IN SEPTEMBER 2002 RESULTING IN IMMEDIATE INJURY. THE POLICY CHANGES OCCURRED IN JULY 2002, PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED. THE COURT ADDS TO TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED; AND, DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED AS TO WEISBERG DECLARATION, EXHIBITS 7, AND, 8, OF PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE. (PLAINTIFF MAY RE-SUBMIT EXHIBIT 41 TO WEISBERG DECLARATION, BECAUSE OF TECHNICAL ERROR) ALSO, THE COURT ADDS: THERE IS TWO MILLION IN TOTAL COVERAGE, PRIMARY ONE MILLION AND UMBRELLA ONE MILLION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS FILED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RICK GRIMSLEY MOTION TO STRIKE COSTS. Appearances: William S. Weisberg, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller (415) 296-7070, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Olivia J. Bradbury, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP (415) 986-5900, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike denied. Defendants are the prevailing parties and entitled to their costs. Defendants consistently stated that plaintiff was only entitled to Two million dollars and plaintiff obtained nothing from this action. The challenged costs appear reasonable. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY, and, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, AND, OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Marc G. Fishleder, Esq. of King, King & Fishleder, (510) 844-3400, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; William S. Weisberg, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller (415) 296-7070, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Tom Trombadore, Esq. of the Law Offices of J. Thomas Trombadore, (415) 439-8373, appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; and, Arthur Schwartz, Esq., Olivia Bradbury Esq., and, Douglas Scullion, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, (415) 986-5900, appearing for defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. The 2002-2003 policies are the only policies at issue. The trigger of coverage was the single event of the accident in September 2002 resulting in immediate injury. The policy changes occurred in July 2002, prior to the accident. No triable issue of fact is raised. The Court adds to tentative ruling: Plaintiff's objections to evidence are overruled; and, Defendants' objections are sustained as to Weisberg Declaration, Exhibits 7, and, 8, of Plaintiff's evidence. (Plaintiff may re-submit Exhibit 41 to Weisberg Declaration, because of technical error) Also, the Court adds: there is two million in total coverage, primary one million and umbrella one million. Plaintiff's objections filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY AND TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: MARC G. FISHLEDER, ESQUIRE (KING, KING & FISHLEDER, 510.844.3414) AND WILLIAM S. WEISBERG, ESQUIRE (WEISBERG & MILLER, 415.296.7070) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. ARTHUR SCHWARTZ, ESQUIRE (GORDON & REES, 415.296.7070) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY PROPER BUT IT IS DENIED BECAUSE IT REQUIRES THE COURT TO DETERMINE THE NATURE OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES AND THE APPLICABLE INSURANCE POLICY/POLICIES AND THESE ARE ISSUES BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE PLEADINGS. COURT'S RULINGS ON DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT DECLINES TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE INSURANCE POLICIES. THE ISSUE DEFENDANT SEEKS TO RAISE IS BEST ADDRESSED BY A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED IN THE TULARE COUNTY ACTION. COMBINED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES, CSR 12365

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Regarding Plntf'S Document'S Req.'S, Set One
Off Calendar Dec-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Douglas Scullion, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Mid-Century Insurance Company
William Weisberg, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Rick W. Grimsley
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Russell R. Mckinney, Esq., and Tyler Offenhauser, Esq., for Joinder defendants: Andrew Piperis, Armondo Apodaca, Andrew Piperis, Inc. and Bremer Whyte Brown & O'meara
Court Reporter: Renee Sera, Telephone (415)664-4269

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION.
1) Defendant Mid-Century Insurance Company's motion for a protective order regarding request for production of documents (set #1) is granted in part.
The Court finds that the assignment neither waived the attorney client or attorney work product privilege nor created a right of inspection without limitation. The Court also finds that the exception set forth in Evidence Code Section 962 does not apply. If defendant intends to withhold production of any documents, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log.
MODIFICATION: THAT COMPLIANCE BY DECEMBER 13, 2007 AND PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED DOCUMENTS AND COPIES SHALL BE RETURNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
